fault_hypothesis: The rate limit check on line 71 is incorrect. It uses `len(recent) > limit` instead of `len(recent) >= limit`, which fails to block a new email when the number of recent sends is exactly equal to the limit.

constraints.intent: The decision to block or allow an email is only correct when the number of recent sends is not exactly equal to the limit.
If the logic proceeds to allow an email, the number of recent sends must be strictly less than the limit.
If an email is allowed (the first element of the returned tuple is True), then there must be more than zero sending slots remaining (the second element is > 0).
